title: Throttle daily podcast retries on upstream writer failures
description: Add bounded retry backoff for both fp-digest and the-rundown jobs so intermittent upstream LLM failures do not hammer Anthropic/opencode every 10 seconds. Reuse existing process_after scheduling and keep collection reuse behavior unchanged.
close_reason: Both daily podcast jobs now use bounded retry backoff via process_after, with failure state tracked in the DB and consumer retry logs showing next scheduled attempt.

title: Preserve publish-script source artifacts and produce dog cancer vaccine deep-dive
description: Preserve source script and show-notes artifacts for future publish-script/deep-dives episodes, and use that capability to produce a new deep-dives-style episode about the dog cancer vaccine story. Current implementation path: pipeline/script_processor.py plus the publish-script CLI in pipeline/__main__.py. Existing limitation: published metadata is stored, but the source script/show-notes artifacts are not persisted under /persist/my-podcasts/scripts, which blocks reliable re-publishing, auditing, and follow-on deep dives. Expected follow-up work should add durable persistence for those source artifacts, confirm it integrates with the deep-dives feed that already exists, and create/publish the dog cancer vaccine episode using the preserved-artifact workflow.
notes: Starting point for future handoff: investigate how pipeline/script_processor.py and the publish-script entrypoint in pipeline/__main__.py handle incoming script/show-notes inputs, then add persistence of those source artifacts under /persist/my-podcasts/scripts alongside existing published metadata. Second deliverable is a new deep-dives-style episode for the dog cancer vaccine story, published via the existing deep-dives feed using the new artifact-preservation path.
close_reason: Implemented auto-archival of source scripts for publish-script episodes and published dog cancer deep-dive to deep-dives feed. Verified: 16/16 tests pass, DB row present, archived files in /persist/my-podcasts/scripts/deep-dives/, live feed at podcast.mohrbacher.dev/feeds/deep-dives.xml shows both episodes.

title: ttsjoin cache can be poisoned by partial mp3 writes on process kill
description:
## Risk

`joinery/api/openai.py::OpenAIApi.process_to_file` uses `client.audio.speech.with_streaming_response.create(...).stream_to_file(file_path)`. The destination file is written incrementally. If the Python process is killed mid-stream (OOM, SIGTERM, systemd restart, etc.), the cache file is left partially written. ttsjoin's cache-hit check is `if not file_path.exists()` — a truncated mp3 still 'exists', so the next run reuses it forever.

The partial mp3 may still decode successfully via pydub (mp3 is framed; a truncated mp3 typically plays up to the last complete frame) and concatenate cleanly into the final output, producing a build that is shorter than the script without any error surfacing.

## Evidence

- `openai.py` line 40-43: no atomic-write pattern, no size check, no minimum-duration assertion on the downloaded file.
- `base.py::get_file_path`: cache key is `md5(text)`. No fingerprint of expected audio duration/size, so there's no way to detect a truncated cache file without re-fetching.
- ttsjoin's own tests only assert `len(audio) > 0`.

## Plausible symptom

This is a candidate explanation for the listener-reported Levine 2026-04-22 silent gap at 18:15-20:35. The currently-live mp3 has no silence — but if a prior run left a truncated chunk in cache and the listener's podcast client downloaded that build before a subsequent rerun produced the clean build we see now, they'd hear the gap. Not confirmed; see my-podcasts-dae for context.

## Mitigations we can apply locally

1. Post-TTS silence-gate (see the fix for my-podcasts-dae) will catch truncated chunks that decode as silence OR short chunks that cause the final duration to fall below an expected floor.
2. Wrap ttsjoin with a cache-dir hygiene pass: before invoking ttsjoin, delete any cache files whose size is below a plausible floor (say, <50 KB for a chunk <4096 chars — a 4096-char chunk at 32 kbps mp3 is ~1 MB).
3. Atomic-write patch upstream: stream to a `.part` file and rename on success. This is a small PR worth sending to drien/tts-joinery even though the repo is dormant. See upstream dormancy notes below.

## Upstream status (2026-04-23)

- drien/tts-joinery v1.0.5 is the latest release (2025-03-20).
- HEAD == v1.0.5. No unreleased fixes.
- Last commit a year ago. Repo appears unmaintained.
- No open or closed issues mentioning silent chunks, partial writes, cache invalidation, or output validation.
- Only merged PRs are unrelated (4o-mini-tts support, an unrelated CACHE_DIR hotfix).

So any fix lives on our side unless/until upstream accepts a patch.

## Related

- my-podcasts-dae (FP 4/23 silence) — different mechanism (API returned silent 200) but the shared pipeline fix (post-TTS silence gate) covers both.
- my-podcasts-csn (duplicate episode rows) — independent.

title: Document daily podcast hardening and incident response
description: Add repo docs for the daily podcast hardening work: top-level AGENTS TOC updates, pipeline-specific AGENTS quick start, and operational skills covering monitoring, retries, empty-output hardening, and current manual recovery flow.
close_reason: Added AGENTS navigation, pipeline runbook, and skills for daily podcast hardening and incident response.

title: Add admin CLI to inspect and reset errored daily jobs
description: Add a small jobs CLI surface for listing errored fp-digest / the-rundown jobs and resetting them back to pending, clearing stale work artifacts by default. Follow up by documenting the operational flow in a repo skill.
close_reason: Completed admin CLI for listing and resetting errored daily podcast jobs, plus operator skill and AGENTS.md discovery docs

title: Daily podcasts produce duplicate episodes table rows seconds apart
description:
## Symptom

Today's and yesterday's daily podcast runs each produced two rows in the `episodes` table with the same slug and r2_key but different durations and sizes, created seconds apart:

| feed_slug | slug | row 1 created_at / duration | row 2 created_at / duration |
|---|---|---|---|
| fp-digest | 2026-04-23-fp-digest | 08:37:23 / 682s / 2726828B | 08:37:40 / 735s / 2941388B |
| fp-digest | 2026-04-22-fp-digest | 08:36:46 / 859s / 3434636B | 08:36:56 / 884s / 3536684B |
| the-rundown | 2026-04-23-the-rundown | 08:39:36 / 664s / 2658188B | 08:39:37 / 664s / 2658284B |
| the-rundown | 2026-04-22-the-rundown | 08:34:58 / 694s / 2776460B | 08:35:29 / 703s / 2812076B |

(Taken from state.sqlite3 episodes table, created_at UTC.)

Levine 2026-04-22 had only one row. The daily jobs (fp-digest, the-rundown) consistently get duplicated; email-triggered runs seem to not.

## Why this matters

- Both rows point at the same r2_key, so the second TTS run overwrites the first. The feed serves whatever was written last.
- This plausibly relates to the FP 2026-04-23 silence bug (my-podcasts-dae): if two TTS runs are racing, one could produce clean audio and the other silent audio, and we publish whichever lost the race to finish.
- The duration/size differences confirm they are separate TTS invocations, not a double-insert of the same mp3.

## Consumer log evidence (Apr 23)

Apr 23 04:34:20 '...Preparing to run TTS in 3 chunks...([4084, 4074, 2736])' (FP, pid 3426050)
Apr 23 04:37:17 FP 'Processing audio files' / 'Finalizing'
Apr 23 04:37:30 parent consumer 'Completed FP digest: 13ab6bed-...'
Apr 23 04:37:40 parent consumer 'Processing Rundown job with script: 62894088-...'

The parent log shows only ONE FP digest invocation, yet two episode rows exist 17 seconds apart (08:37:23 and 08:37:40 UTC = 04:37:23 and 04:37:40 ET). 04:37:23 is during 'Processing audio files'; 04:37:40 is right when the Rundown starts. The second FP row is suspicious — was something else writing episode rows for the fp-digest feed_slug?

## Investigation starting points

1. Check `pipeline/consumer.py` for any path that could re-insert an FP episode after `process_fp_digest_job` returns. Same for `process_rundown_job`.
2. Check whether `regenerate_and_upload_feed` or any downstream step re-invokes the processor.
3. Check whether there's a second consumer loop iteration that picks up a job already marked completed but not yet committed (race between `mark_fp_digest_completed` and the next `list_due_fp_digest`).
4. Check whether something is calling `store.insert_episode` twice (e.g., retry logic after a partial failure).
5. Check whether a separate systemd timer / CLI invocation is running the same pipeline in parallel.

## Hypothesis: work_dir reuse race

The consumer's 'reuse prior collection' path (consumer.py ~line 469) lets a retry reuse collection_done.json + plan.json. If the first run actually succeeded but didn't mark the job completed before the consumer loop came around again, the second iteration would re-run TTS (different randomness / different OpenAI result → different duration) and re-publish. This would also explain why only daily jobs show this pattern and email-driven Levine/Yglesias runs don't.

## Remediation

Dedupe the episodes table by (feed_slug, slug) keeping the most recent row, then fix the double-invoke.

title: FP Digest 2026-04-23 has 3:26 of silence at end of mp3
description:
## Symptom

The published mp3 at https://podcast.mohrbacher.dev/episodes/fp-digest/2026-04-23-fp-digest.mp3 contains 3 minutes 26 seconds of pure digital silence (−91 dB, silencedetect at −40 dB / 5 s catches it immediately) at the end, from timestamp 8:49 (529s) through 12:15 (735s). Reported via listener feedback.

Speech ends mid-script, right at the end of chunk 2 of 3. The script (at /persist/my-podcasts/scripts/fp-digest/2026-04-23.txt, 10914 chars, 1806 words) covers Strait of Hormuz (chunk 1), Gaza UN numbers + Haiti (chunks 1-2), and FBI/national-labs wrap-up + sign-off (chunk 3). Chunk 3 is what went silent. Listener reported silence starts at '...Trump said last week it was pretty serious stuff. Now [silence]' which matches the paragraph 27/28 boundary in the script — roughly the 72% mark — exactly the chunk-2/chunk-3 join.

The file was generated, sized, uploaded to R2, and marked complete with no error logged. Consumer log (Apr 23 04:34:20) shows 'Preparing to run TTS in 3 chunks...([4084, 4074, 2736])' followed by a normal 'Processing audio files' / 'Finalizing' sequence.

## Root cause hypothesis

OpenAI TTS API occasionally returns an mp3 of the correct approximate duration that decodes as digital silence. ttsjoin concatenates whatever comes back without validating audio content. pipeline/fp_processor.py (and the parallel processors: things_happen_processor.py, processor.py) then upload and publish without any audio-content validation.

## Evidence

- ffmpeg silencedetect on the live mp3: silence_start 529.09, silence_end 735.24, silence_duration 206.15
- Volume sampling confirms −91 dB flatline from 530s onward
- FP Digest 4/22 (same pipeline, same voice=onyx) has no silence
- Consumer log shows no TTS error

## Fix direction

See sibling issue for proposed silence-gate in the TTS processors.

## Remediation for this episode

Re-run the FP digest job for 2026-04-23 so the mp3 is regenerated from the cached script. The script is on disk at /persist/my-podcasts/scripts/fp-digest/2026-04-23.txt and the work dir should still be at /tmp/fp-digest-13ab6bed-6e7c-4e9d-8c42-773558ce4d2a/ (check before re-running). May need to clear the ttsjoin cache entry for the silent chunk first, otherwise rerun will hit cache and reproduce the bug.

## Related

- See the other bug filed today about duplicate episodes table rows for FP Digest and The Rundown — may be related or independent.

title: Fix FP digest empty writer output retries
close_reason: Writer-stage validation now rejects empty FP output before TTS, with 300s timeout and regression coverage.

title: Cap daily podcast retries and mark exhausted jobs errored
description: Add a max retry limit for fp-digest and the-rundown jobs so prolonged upstream outages stop retrying forever. After the cap, mark the job errored, keep the last error, and stop scheduling new attempts until manually reset or requeued.
close_reason: Daily podcast retries now stop after the 12-hour retry budget is exhausted and jobs are marked errored with the final failure recorded.

title: Refactor source-specific processing with adapters and add Yglesias title format
description: Refactor pipeline source-specific branching into a maintainable adapter architecture. Current processor behavior includes multiple feed-specific conditionals for Levine and Yglesias (title formatting, body cleanup, source URL extraction). Implement a pluggable source adapter layer and migrate existing behaviors into adapters. Include requested Yglesias title format: 'YYYY-MM-DD - Slow Boring - <Title>'.
acceptance_criteria: Core processor has no feed_slug if/elif branches for source-specific logic; Levine and Yglesias behaviors are preserved or improved; New Yglesias title format is active for newly processed episodes; Tests cover adapter behaviors and regressions.
notes: Refactor shipped end-to-end. Core processor no longer has feed_slug if/elif branches for source-specific logic.

title: Define source adapter interface and wiring points in processor
description: Design a small source adapter contract for newsletter-specific behavior. Proposed methods: format_title(date_str, subject_raw, subject_slug), clean_body(raw_email, body), extract_source_url(raw_email, date_str, subject_raw), and optional metadata hooks. Document where the adapter is resolved and invoked in process_email_bytes.
acceptance_criteria: Adapter interface documented in code comments/design notes; processor wiring points identified; no behavior changes yet.
notes: Completed: introduced source adapter contract and registry in pipeline/source_adapters.py; processor wiring now resolves adapter by feed_slug and delegates title, body cleanup, and source URL extraction.

title: Implement adapter registry and migrate Levine/Yglesias logic out of processor conditionals
description: Create source adapter modules (default, levine, yglesias), register by feed_slug/preset key, and move source-specific logic from process_email_bytes into adapter implementations. Keep behavior parity for existing Levine URL extraction and Yglesias body cleanup/source URL extraction.
acceptance_criteria: process_email_bytes delegates source-specific work to adapters; existing output parity validated on representative sample emails.
notes: Completed: migrated Levine and Yglesias source-specific logic out of processor conditionals into LevineAdapter and YglesiasAdapter.

title: Add Yglesias title format: date + Slow Boring + post title
description: Implement Yglesias-specific title formatter in the adapter so new episodes are titled 'YYYY-MM-DD - Slow Boring - <Title>'. Ensure raw subject normalization strips newsletter prefixes where needed while preserving meaningful punctuation/casing.
acceptance_criteria: New Yglesias episodes use requested title format; Levine title behavior unchanged; unit tests cover formatting edge cases.
notes: Completed: Yglesias title format now 'YYYY-MM-DD - Slow Boring - <Title>' for new episodes via YglesiasAdapter.format_title.

title: Regression tests, rollout verification, and docs update for adapter refactor
description: Expand tests to cover adapter dispatch and source behaviors (Levine and Yglesias). Run full suite and lint. Verify one live newsletter per source post-refactor to ensure queue->audio->feed path still works. Update AGENTS/skill docs if operational behavior changes.
acceptance_criteria: Tests pass; no regression in feed/audio outputs; docs updated for any changed operator expectations.
notes: Completed: updated tests for adapter behavior and source URL/cleanup; uv run ruff check pipeline and uv run pytest both passing (24 tests). Restarted my-podcasts-consumer.

title: Harden Rundown writer against empty LLM output
description: Add the same empty-script guard used in fp_writer to rundown_writer so zero-byte writer output cannot silently reach downstream stages if the LLM returns only tags/whitespace.
close_reason: Rundown writer now rejects empty LLM output before downstream processing, mirroring FP hardening.
